alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained localisation part of the network is independently useful as it learns to fit a 3D morphable model to a single image. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The localiser can be trained using only simple geometric loss functions on a relatively small dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The localiser can be trained using only simple geometric loss functions on a relatively small dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The localiser is able to perform robust normalisation on highly uncontrolled images including occlusion, self-occlusion, and large pose changes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method to automatically adjust camera exposure for image processing and computer vision applications on mobile robot platforms is introduced.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Visual features account for performance similar to that achieved by using topological measurements.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The coefficient of variation of interspike intervals depends on the relative proportion of thermal and threshold noise, where threshold noise is the dominant source in the current experimental demonstrations.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about the scaling behavior of arbitrarily shrinking the time-step; this specific limitation appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial neural network successfully maps out the quantum phase diagram of disordered topological superconductor in class DIII. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The artificial neural network successfully maps out the quantum phase diagram of disordered topological superconductors in class DIII. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same in both versions; pluralization and minor wording differences do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification of phases by the artificial neural network is consistent with calculations from the transfer matrix method or noncommutative geometry approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phases classified by the artificial neural network are consistent with calculations from the transfer matrix method or noncommutative geometry approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same consistency between ANN classifications and established computation methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine can classify the $\text{Z}_2$, trivial, and thermal metal phases with high confidence over the entire phase diagram when all three phases are present. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine can classify the Z2, trivial, and thermal metal phases with high confidence over the entire phase diagram when all three phases are present. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about classification performance is equivalent in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine remains confused in certain regions when only the $\text{Z}_2$ and trivial phases are present, indicating the detection of the unknown thermal metal phase. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine remains confused in a certain region when only the Z2 and trivial phases are present, leading to the detection of an unknown phase identified as the thermal metal phase. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the preprint wording to state that the confusion led to identification of the unknown phase as the thermal metal; this is a clarification rather than a reversal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method currently uses only the first moment of the quasiparticle distribution for input, but can be applied to a wider variety of systems by including higher moments. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present an equivalent forward-looking contribution about extending inputs to higher moments, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-level feature fusion method improves the accuracy for small objects in the Single Shot Multibox Detector (SSD). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-level feature fusion method improves the accuracy for small objects in the Single Shot Multibox Detector (SSD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Textual content is effectively identical between preprint and publication; claim meaning and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two feature fusion modules achieve higher mean Average Precision (mAP) on PASCALVOC2007 than the baseline SSD by 1.6 and 1.7 points respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concatenation and element-sum feature fusion modules achieve higher mean Average Precision (mAP) on PASCAL VOC2007 than the baseline SSD by 1.6 and 1.7 points respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication names the two modules explicitly (concatenation and element-sum), clarifying but not changing the substance or scope of the numerical result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The testing speed of the proposed fusion modules is 43 and 40 FPS, which is superior to the state of the art Deconvolutional single shot detector (DSSD) by 29.4 and 26.4 FPS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The testing speed of the proposed fusion modules is 43 and 40 FPS, which is superior to the Deconvolutional single shot detector (DSSD) by 29.4 and 26.4 FPS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numbers and comparison are identical; no substantive change in claim content or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed dynamic updating framework improves correction efficiency by allowing highly transcriber-adaptive cost modeling. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dynamic updating framework improves correction efficiency by allowing highly transcriber-adaptive cost modeling. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical wording and meaning between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A realistic user study observed efficiency improvements of 15% on average and 42% for participants who deviated most from the initial cost model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efficiency improvements of 15% on average were observed in a realistic user study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the combined preprint result into two claims; this mapped match focuses on the average 15% figure, which narrows the scope relative to the combined preprint phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The updating framework can capture dynamically changing factors, such as transcriber fatigue and topic familiarity, which significantly influence the transcriber's working behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The updating framework can capture dynamically changing factors, such as transcriber fatigue and topic familiarity, which significantly influence the transcriber’s working behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and meaning are effectively identical, retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Long Short Term Memory Fully Convolutional Network (LSTM-FCN) achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to others. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed long short term memory fully convolutional network (LSTM-FCN) achieves state-of-the-art performance in time series classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim; publication adds the domain (time series classification) but does not change the substantive claim of state-of-the-art performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The augmentation of fully convolutional networks with LSTM RNN sub-modules significantly enhances performance with a nominal increase in model size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Augmenting fully convolutional networks with LSTM RNN sub-modules significantly enhances their performance with minimal increase in model size. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording minorly differs (nominal vs minimal) and publication frames as a contribution, but the claim's substance about performance improvement and small size impact is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilization of the attention mechanism in the Attention Long Short Term Memory Fully Convolutional Network (ALSTM-FCN) allows for visualization of the decision process of the LSTM cell. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention mechanism improves time series classification and allows visualization of the decision process of the LSTM cell. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds an explicit claim that attention improves classification (beyond visualization), broadening and changing the semantic content of the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning is proposed as a method to enhance the performance of trained models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Refinement is proposed as a method to enhance the performance of trained models. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different wording (refinement vs fine-tuning) and lower reported certainty; the intent is the same but confidence is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (STGCN) framework effectively captures comprehensive spatio-temporal correlations in traffic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (STGCN) framework effectively captures comprehensive spatio-temporal correlations in traffic data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STGCN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on various real-world traffic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STGCN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on various real-world traffic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and confidence in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model enables much faster training speed with fewer parameters compared to traditional methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model enables much faster training speed with fewer parameters compared to traditional methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim about training speed and parameter efficiency is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Very deep convolutional neural networks show that the deeper the model, the better the classification accuracy will be obtained. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best results in food recognition have been obtained using very deep Convolutional Neural Networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Very deep neural networks may suffer from the overfitting problem. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Very deep neural networks may suffer from the overfitting problem. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of multiple classifiers based on different convolutional models achieves an improvement in performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combination of multiple classifiers based on Convolutional models achieves an improvement in performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach outperforms the independent CNN models on the Food-101 and Food-11 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach outperforms independent Convolutional Neural Networks models on the Food-101 and Food-11 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We define syntactic profiling as a problem of clustering strings based on syntactic similarity, followed by identifying patterns that succinctly describe each cluster.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not restate this explicit definitional contribution; the paper focuses on the synthesis technique and implementation rather than the formal definition present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a technique for synthesizing syntactic profiles that allows for interactive refinement by requesting a desired number of clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a technique for synthesizing syntactic profiles that allows for interactive refinement by requesting a desired number of clusters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Across 153 tasks over 75 large real datasets, we observe a median profiling time of only approximately 0.7 seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Across 153 tasks over 75 large real datasets, we observe a median profiling time of only ∼ 0.7s.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Access to syntactic profiles may allow for more accurate synthesis of programs using fewer examples in programming-by-example workflows such as FlashFill.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Access to syntactic profiles may allow for more accurate synthesis of programs in programming-by-example workflows such as Flash Fill.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed two-stage approach effectively organizes information in video surveillance systems by detecting faces and grouping sequences of frames.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Gender and age are accurately estimated for each cluster of faces to enhance the usability of the organized video collection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that matching the normalized average of feature vectors from all frames in a track yields the most accurate and fast solution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework predicts road friction levels using historical friction data and weather station data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Several novel metrics are introduced to evaluate the performance of explanations quantitatively without human involvement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper revisits portfolio optimization problems focusing on minimizing/maximizing investment risk and investment concentration under specific constraints.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of the optimization problems is conducted using the Lagrange multiplier method and the random matrix approach.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An automatic system for food analysis can improve understanding of nutritional information and assist in dietary corrections.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms for semi-supervised discriminant analysis (SDA) achieve good predictive performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks are intrinsically affected by the problem of overgeneralization due to their partitioning of the full input space into a fixed set of target classes used during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks are intrinsically affected by the problem of overgeneralization due to their partitioning of the full input space into a fixed set of target classes used during training.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel inputs belonging to categories unknown during training can fool the system into classifying them as one of the known classes, even with a high degree of confidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel inputs belonging to categories unknown during training can fool neural networks into classifying them as one of the known classes, even with a high degree of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for computing a confidence score using denoising autoencoders can correctly identify regions of the input space close to the training distribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improving the handling of overgeneralization in neural networks may enhance the security of these systems in critical applications.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not restate this higher-level security-consequence claim, focusing instead on empirical tests.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution presented in this paper is tested on benchmarks of 'fooling', open set recognition, and 1-class recognition constructed from the MNIST and Fashion-MNIST datasets.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach jointly extracts and stores all attributes of the event as a single representation (quintuple).

alignments.rationale: The publication's claims concern explainability in computer vision and a book topic; there is no matching claim about CGNN or causal modeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CGNN effectively identifies v-structures and conditional independences in causal modeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains no statements about v-structures or conditional independences; the preprint claim does not appear in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CGNN orients edges in a skeleton to uncover the directed acyclic causal graph of a set of random variables.
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding claim about orienting causal skeletons or DAG recovery in the publication, so this claim is removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CGNN is extended to handle confounders, incorporating latent variables into the causal model.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not discuss extensions for confounders or latent variables; the preprint contribution is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phrase-based Statistical models perform optimally in terms of both translation quality and complexity of the system.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert a general optimality claim for phrase-based models; this specific statement is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution generates unseen morphological forms and injects them into original training corpora.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Injecting unseen morphological forms into the original training corpus improves the quality of translation in terms of adequacy and fluency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach predicts the distribution of human opinion scores using a convolutional neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach predicts the distribution of human opinion scores using a convolutional neural network.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our architecture is significantly simpler than other methods while maintaining comparable performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our resulting network can score images reliably and with high correlation to human perception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our resulting network can score images reliably and with high correlation to human perception.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method allows for single-image, semantic- and perceptually-aware, no-reference quality assessment.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed technique efficiently synthesizes images of atmospheric clouds using a combination of Monte Carlo integration and neural networks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method represents a viable solution for applications such as cloud design and high-quality production of animated content due to its temporal stability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a novel taxonomy of fine-grained 'dialogue acts' frequently observed in customer service that is more suited to the domain than existing taxonomies.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We present actionable rules based on our findings that can be used as guidelines for outcome-driven automated customer service platforms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semi-automated system demonstrated an accuracy of 72% in correctly sizing a nasal PAP mask. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The semi-automated system demonstrated an accuracy of 72% in correctly sizing a nasal PAP mask. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system achieved 96% accuracy in sizing to within 1 mask size group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieved 96% accuracy in sizing to within 1 mask size group. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital–analog decomposition of the master equation leads to quantum synchronization between both two‐level systems.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert quantum synchronization between two-level systems; the publication frames the result in terms of the digital–analog decomposition, thus clarifying the mechanism behind the synchronization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The digital-analog block decomposition identifies fundamental elements of a quantum machine learning protocol.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate that the decomposition 'identifies fundamental elements' of the protocol; that specific framing is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed quantum machine learning protocol enhances the synchronization process despite different loss/decoherence mechanisms in each subsystem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical simulations show that the proposed quantum machine learning protocol enhances the synchronization process despite different loss/decoherence mechanisms in each subsystem.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (with numerical simulations) about enhancement of synchronization under differing loss/decoherence, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the protocol can be based on current technologies in superconducting circuits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed implementation of the quantum machine learning protocol is based on current technologies in superconducting circuits.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the implementation is based on current superconducting-circuit technologies with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A powerful and flexible quantum machine learning protocol emerges when the algorithm is equipped with a classical feedback mechanism.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a claim about a classical feedback mechanism producing a powerful, flexible protocol that is not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mixed-effects model learns a typical group-average trajectory characterizing the propagation of measurement changes across the graph nodes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mixed-effects model learns a typical group-average trajectory characterizing the propagation of measurement changes across the graph nodes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about learning a group-average trajectory.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model estimates the variability of spatiotemporal patterns within the group through subject-specific trajectories defined via spatial and temporal transformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model estimates the variability of spatiotemporal patterns within the group through subject-specific trajectories defined via spatial and temporal transformations.
alignments.rationale: The claim about estimating variability via subject-specific spatial and temporal transformations is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model describes the propagation of cortical atrophy during the course of Alzheimer's Disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model describes the propagation of cortical atrophy during the course of Alzheimer’s Disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim that the model describes cortical atrophy propagation in AD.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model parameters show the variability of the average pattern of atrophy in terms of trajectories across brain regions, age at disease onset, and pace of propagation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model parameters show the variability of the average pattern of atrophy in terms of trajectories across brain regions, age at disease onset, and pace of propagation.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's characterization of parameter-driven variability in atrophy patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The personalization of this model yields accurate prediction of maps of cortical thickness in patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The personalization of this model yields accurate prediction of maps of cortical thickness in patients.
alignments.rationale: The predictive accuracy of personalized cortical-thickness maps is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture, J-MOD$^{2}$, jointly estimates depth and detects obstacles without the need to compute a global map.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed J-MOD2 architecture jointly learns to detect obstacles and estimate their depth for MAV flight applications.
alignments.rationale: Both claim joint obstacle detection and depth estimation for MAVs; the publication emphasizes the MAV application while the map-related detail is addressed separately in another publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The joint information from obstacle detection and depth estimation increases the robustness of both tasks to scenario changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture increases the robustness of both obstacle detection and depth estimation tasks by jointly exploiting the information learned from each task.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the robustness benefit more strongly (higher certainty) and explicitly attributes it to joint exploitation of learned information, amplifying the preprint's medium-confidence result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of J-MOD$^{2}$ compared to state-of-the-art multi-task methods for semantic segmentation and depth estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of J-MOD2 compared to state-of-the-art multitask methods for semantic segmentation and depth estimation.
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim is carried over directly and matches the publication's reported comparisons to state-of-the-art methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of J-MOD$^{2}$ in a full system allows a MAV to explore unknown scenarios and plan safe trajectories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: J-MOD2 does not require the computation of a global map while maintaining compatibility with a global SLAM system if needed.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader system-level claim about exploration and safe trajectory planning is reduced in the publication to an implementation-level detail about map-free operation and SLAM compatibility, narrowing the scope of the original systems claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel cross-modal LSTM (X-LSTM) demonstrates superiority over baseline approaches in predicting weight objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel cross-modal LSTM (X-LSTM) demonstrates superiority over baseline approaches in predicting weight objectives.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same superiority claim as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The X-LSTM improves parameter efficiency by processing each modality separately and allowing for information flow between them.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The X-LSTM improves parameter efficiency by processing each modality separately and allowing for information flow between them.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about parameter efficiency and modality-specific processing is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general hyperparameter optimisation technique for X-LSTMs significantly improves performance compared to LSTM and a prior state-of-the-art cross-modal approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general hyperparameter optimisation technique for X-LSTMs significantly improves performance compared to LSTM and a prior state-of-the-art cross-modal approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result about hyperparameter optimisation improving performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's predictions reveal implications about latent variables in the task of predicting weight objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's predictions reveal implications about latent variables in the task of predicting weight objectives.
alignments.rationale: The observation regarding implications for latent variables is presented unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article provides a comprehensive survey of the salient modelling methods found in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article provides a comprehensive survey of the salient modelling methods found in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's claim of providing a comprehensive survey.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A variety of modelling approaches exist that vary widely in their methodology and underlying assumptions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A variety of modelling approaches exist that vary widely in their methodology and underlying assumptions.
alignments.rationale: The claim about the diversity of modelling approaches is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The different modelling approaches cater to the needs of various sub-communities and reflect different practical uses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The different modelling approaches cater to the needs of various sub-communities and reflect different practical uses.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about catering to different sub-communities and uses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article discusses open problems that may form the basis for fruitful future research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article discusses open problems that may form the basis for fruitful future research.
alignments.rationale: The discussion of open problems for future research is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes a representation of prioritized normative reasoning by argumentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper proposes a representation of prioritized normative reasoning by argumentation using hierarchical abstract normative systems (HANS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but adds the specific formalism (HANS), clarifying the technical setting without changing the claim's intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three kinds of prioritized normative reasoning approaches are defined: Greedy, Reduction, and Optimization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three kinds of prioritized normative reasoning approaches called Greedy, Reduction, and Optimization are defined. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same three defined approaches with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For a totally ordered hierarchical abstract normative system, Greedy and Reduction can be represented in argumentation using the weakest link and the last link principles respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For a totally ordered HANS, Greedy and Reduction can be represented in argumentation by applying the weakest link and the last link principles, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same technical result with equivalent terminology (HANS) and principles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimization can be represented by introducing additional defeats that reject arguments containing norms not belonging to the maximal obeyable set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimization can be represented by introducing additional defeats that capture the idea that arguments containing norms not belonging to the maximal obeyable set should be rejected. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same representation for Optimization with slightly more explicit wording, clarifying the mechanism without changing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces the concepts of single-core and multi-core inhomogeneous classes of objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces the concepts of single-core and multi-core inhomogeneous classes of objects, which allow simultaneous defining of different types within one class. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the introduction of the concepts with their purpose (allowing simultaneous definition), expanding the original terse contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed extensions of the class allow simultaneous defining of different types within one class of objects, avoiding duplication of properties and methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed extensions of the class decrease sizes of program codes and provide more efficient information storage in databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the benefits in terms of reduced code size and storage efficiency; this is a clarified/operationalized statement of avoiding duplication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data storage in a relational database using the proposed extensions is more efficient in some cases compared to using homogeneous classes of objects. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show that data storage in a relational database using the proposed extensions is more efficient than using homogeneous classes of objects in some cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same empirical claim, adding that it is supported by experimental results but not changing the claimed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel deep-learning-based detection methods yield high accuracy in detecting events from time series seismic signals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our techniques yield high accuracy in detecting events from seismic signals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same high-accuracy detection result using the proposed deep-learning techniques.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cascaded region-based convolutional neural network effectively captures earthquake events of varying durations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel cascaded region-based convolutional neural network to capture earthquake events in different sizes while incorporating contextual information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a stronger statement (higher certainty) and adds that contextual information is incorporated, clarifying and slightly amplifying the original method claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detection problem is formulated as a learning-from-noise problem to address issues with incorrectly annotated positive events. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We formulate the detection problem as a learning-from-noise problem to address issues with incorrectly annotated positive events. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same formulation and motivation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our methods can potentially serve as powerful tools for locating events from time series data in various applications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel deep-learning-based detection methods can potentially be powerful tools for identifying events from time series data in various applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same concluding claim but with increased stated confidence (medium vs low).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed tensor-based classification model reduces the number of parameters required, thereby decreasing the number of training samples needed for proper training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed classification approach significantly reduces the number of weight parameters required to train the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes parameter reduction but omits the explicit causal claim about reducing required training samples, narrowing and tempering the original linked claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed classification model provides a physical interpretation regarding the model coefficients on the classification output. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed classification approach provides a physical interpretation of model coefficients on the classification output. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same interpretability/physical-interpretation claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rank-1 Feedforward Neural Network (FNN) outperforms state-of-the-art classification methods, particularly when a small number of training samples are available. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the proposed scheme outperforms state-of-the-art classification methods, especially with a small number of training samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same empirical superiority, explicitly noting the advantage in small-sample settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed tensor-based classifiers are capable of dimensionality reduction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to dimensionality reduction; the published claims focus on parameter reduction, coherency, and empirical performance instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Digital histopathological images have led to an increased demand for their analysis using machine learning techniques. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of digital pathological image analysis using machine learning algorithms is introduced. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same topical motivation as an introduction to the application; wording is slightly rephrased but content remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are specific issues related to the analysis of digital pathological images that need to be considered. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are specific issues related to the analysis of digital pathological images that need to be considered. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Possible solutions to the problems associated with digital pathological image analysis using machine learning algorithms are proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Possible solutions to the problems associated with digital pathological image analysis are proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same proposed solutions; wording is effectively identical aside from minor phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Search By Ideal Candidates system improves search efficiency by generating queries based on ideal candidates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Search By Ideal Candidates system improves search efficiency by generating queries based on ideal candidates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim Q0 is effectively identical in content to P0, only minor label differences (contribution vs result) so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed solution in query building and search ranking tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed solution in query building and search ranking tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P1 and Q2 match exactly in content and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ranking features used in traditional Query-By-Keyword systems may not be optimal for the new Query-By-Example paradigm. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transition from Query-By-Keyword to Query-By-Example presents challenges in query generation and ranking model evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same concern more broadly as general challenges in query generation and ranking evaluation, clarifying and broadening the original point about ranking features not being optimal.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new system has been made available to all LinkedIn members as of the writing of this paper. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q3 is a deployment/availability statement not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed reweighted alternating direction method of multipliers solves nonconvex and nonsmooth problems effectively. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reweighted alternating direction method of multipliers solves nonconvex and nonsmooth problems effectively. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 with the same scope and certainty; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All subproblems in the proposed algorithm are convex and easy to solve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All subproblems in the proposed algorithm are convex and easy to solve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P1 and Q1 match directly in content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm globally converges to a critical point of an auxiliary function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm globally converges to a critical point of an auxiliary function. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q2 carries the same technical convergence claim as P2; label change (result->conclusion) does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence guarantees are supported by the Kurdyka-{\text{L}}ojasiewicz property. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convergence guarantees are supported by the Kurdyka-Łojasiewicz property. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates P3 using standard notation; the substance is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed algorithm. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q4 reports numerical demonstration not asserted in the preprint claims list, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cascade of fully convolutional neural networks effectively segments multi-modal Magnetic Resonance images of brain tumors into background and three hierarchical regions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cascade of fully convolutional neural networks effectively segments multi-modal Magnetic Resonance images of brain tumors into background and three hierarchical regions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 repeats the main methodological/conclusion claim of P0 with identical wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieved average Dice scores of 0.7859, 0.9050, and 0.8378 for enhancing tumor core, whole tumor, and tumor core, respectively, on the BraTS 2017 validation set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieved average Dice scores of 0.7859, 0.9050, and 0.8378 for enhancing tumor core, whole tumor, and tumor core, respectively, on the BraTS 2017 validation set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P1 and Q1 are identical numerical results, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The corresponding Dice scores for the BraTS 2017 testing set were 0.7831, 0.8739, and 0.7748 for enhancing tumor core, whole tumor, and tumor core, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The corresponding Dice scores for the BraTS 2017 testing set were 0.7831, 0.8739, and 0.7748 for enhancing tumor core, whole tumor, and tumor core, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P2 and Q2 match exactly in reported test-set results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-grained visual-textual pattern mining boosts categorization performance by discovering discriminative visual-textual pairwise information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fine-grained visual-textual representation learning (VTRL) approach improves categorization performance by discovering discriminative visual-textual pairwise information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 using the VTRL name; the contribution and mechanism are the same, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed visual-textual representation learning approach preserves intra-modality and inter-modality information to generate complementary fine-grained representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VTRL automatically and adaptively discovers discriminative parts through jointly modeling vision and text with generative adversarial networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a different, more specific mechanism (discovering parts via GANs) compared to the preprint's description about preserving intra-/inter-modality information; this represents a substantive revision and a narrower technical focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Textual attention can help discover visual attention in images, indicating its significance in categorization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not present an explicit claim about textual attention discovering visual attention; that preprint claim is not represented and appears omitted in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that the VTRL approach achieves the best categorization accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods on the CUB-200-2011 and Oxford Flowers-102 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 reports comprehensive benchmark results not stated as a claim in the preprint list, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disintegration and Bayesian inversion are fundamental concepts in conditional probability theory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disintegration and Bayesian inversion are fundamental concepts in conditional probability theory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 repeats P0 verbatim in substance; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The abstract graphical formulations of disintegration and Bayesian inversion are used to prove basic results in conditional probability theory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The abstract graphical formulations of disintegration and Bayesian inversion are used to prove basic results in conditional probability theory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P1 and Q1 match directly in content and role in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existence of disintegration and Bayesian inversion is discussed for both discrete probability and measure-theoretic probability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of disintegration and Bayesian inversion is established for both discrete probability and measure-theoretic probability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the language from 'discussed' to 'established', clarifying and amplifying the confidence in the existence results while keeping scope the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usefulness of disintegration and Bayesian inversion is illustrated through several examples. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usefulness of disintegration and Bayesian inversion is illustrated through several examples. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P3 and Q3 are equivalent illustrative claims; unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RefineGAN is a novel deep learning-based generative adversarial model designed for fast and accurate CS-MRI reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RefineGAN is a novel deep learning-based generative adversarial model designed for fast and accurate CS-MRI reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually identical and carries the same contribution claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstruction process using RefineGAN is extremely rapid, taking as low as tens of milliseconds for a 256x256 image. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconstruction process using RefineGAN is extremely rapid, taking as low as tens of milliseconds for the reconstruction of an image. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same runtime claim with equivalent detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RefineGAN achieves superior image quality even at extremely low sampling rates, as low as 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RefineGAN achieves superior image quality even for extremely low sampling rates, as low as 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about superior image quality at 10% sampling is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RefineGAN outperforms state-of-the-art CS-MRI methods by a large margin in terms of both running time and image quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RefineGAN outperforms the state-of-the-art CS-MRI methods by a large margin in terms of both running time and image quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim is carried forward exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method estimates the camera's pose relative to the navigation system with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method estimates the camera's pose relative to the navigation system with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The high-level accuracy claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was able to estimate the pose to within 0.06 m / 1.05° and 0.18 m / 2.39° on two different platforms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was able to estimate the pose to within 0.06 m/1.05 ∘ and 0.18 m/2.39 ∘ on two different platforms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical accuracy results are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm is utilized to estimate the uncertainty of the offset. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm is utilized to estimate the uncertainty of the offset in the camera pose estimation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit context (that the offset is in the camera pose estimation), clarifying but not changing the substance of the MCMC usage.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach involves imaging and manually labelling a calibration pattern to compute a likelihood for estimating the 6D camera pose. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This procedural detail about imaging and manual labelling is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint; it appears as an added methodological detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Around-device interaction techniques can extend the input space using various sensing modalities on mobile and wearable devices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Around-device interaction techniques can extend the input space using various sensing modalities on mobile and wearable devices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The high-level contribution statement is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the feasibility of around-device interaction using corneal reflective imaging based on a high resolution camera. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the feasibility of around-device interaction using corneal reflective imaging based on a high resolution camera. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feasibility result is restated verbatim in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Under optimal conditions, a spatial sensing resolution of 5 cm in the vicinity of a mobile phone is possible. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Under optimal conditions, a spatial sensing resolution of 5 cm in the vicinity of a mobile phone is possible. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantitative sensing-resolution claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed signaling based nanoscale acousto-optic radar and microfluidic particle tracking system demonstrates significant performance for multiple particle tracking. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Monte Carlo simulations demonstrate significant performance for multiple particle tracking with the proposed modulator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert that simulations demonstrate significant multiple-particle-tracking performance; the publication attributes this specifically to the proposed modulator, but the substantive result is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modulator structure combines the advantages of graphene membranes with quantum dots to enhance detection capabilities. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed modulator combines significant advantages of graphene membranes with quantum dots. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same design advantage more assertively (higher certainty) and with slightly stronger wording, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical and Monte-Carlo simulations indicate a signal to noise ratio (SNR) in the range of -7 dB to 10 dB for the tracking system. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieves a signal-to-noise ratio in the range from -7 to 10 dB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical SNR range is the same, but the publication reports it with higher certainty and frames it as an achieved system result, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CSS Tag utilizes classical radar target tracking approaches to identify different bio-particles in nanoscale environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about the CSS Tag and use of classical radar tracking to identify bio-particles does not appear in the published claims and thus was removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A signaling-based nanoscale acousto-optic radar and microfluidic multiple particle tracking system is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly lists the proposed system as a contribution; while the preprint described such a system in context, this specific standalone contribution statement was presented in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system allows for high speed tracking in microfluidic environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about high-speed tracking in microfluidics that was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Augmentation of the weakly annotated training dataset with synthetic images minimizes both the annotation efforts and the cost of capturing images with sufficient variety. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
